summary: Iter 70 backend testing — Biometric-punch geofence gate + 3 new statutory bulk endpoints (PF ECR TXT, ESIC MC CSV, ESIC IP Reg CSV). Created new test file test_iter70_geofence_and_statutory.py with 26 tests, all PASSED. Covers: (1) biometric geofence enforcement — GPS-off employee with selfie+coords inside SKSCO1 geofence → 200 with location_status=inside; same employee outside (~555m away) → 400 with 'outside geofence' detail; firm with office_lat/lng unset → biometric-only punch still works (backward compat), coords cleanly restored after test; GPS-on regression inside 200 / outside 400. (2) All three new endpoints — correct content-type (text/plain / text/csv), correct Content-Disposition filenames (PF_ECR_YYYY-MM.txt, ESIC_MC_YYYY-MM.csv), 404 for unknown run_id, cross-firm 403 for SKSCO1 company_admin on the only foreign run available (csrun_5b9d1e339e75, company_id=None). (3) PF ECR row schema — every non-empty line has exactly 11 hash-separated fields; builder correctly skips rows without a 12-digit UAN or with pf_applicable=False and caps pf_wages at 15000. (4) ESIC MC CSV — 6-column header verbatim; reason_code=0 when days>0 and 7 when zero; rows without esi_ip_no or with esic_applicable=False are skipped. (5) ESIC IP Reg CSV — 17-column header verbatim; only rows with EMPTY esi_ip_no AND esic_applicable=True are included; DOB / DOJ formatted DD/MM/YYYY. Regression suite (iter68 + iter69, 38 tests) still 36 passed / 2 pre-existing env-data skips.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:6010-6022 — the `_bio_with_gps` guard correctly checks BOTH office_lat AND office_lng are non-None before applying the geofence, so a partially-configured firm (only lat, no lng) still gets the legacy biometric-only path. Good defensive coding.

server.py:6013-6019 — the biometric trigger condition is `selfie_base64 OR biometric_method in (face, fingerprint)`. Since biometric_method is REQUIRED on the AttendancePunch model (Literal['fingerprint', 'face'], line 291, no Optional), this OR clause is always true whenever biometric fields are present. Slightly redundant but not incorrect — the intent (bio-only flow) is captured. Non-blocking.

server.py:11534, 11574, 11619 — all three new endpoints use `run.get('company_id') != admin.get('company_id')` for the company_admin cross-firm check. This correctly rejects both foreign-firm runs AND null-company runs (where run.company_id=None, admin.company_id=cmp_xxx). Consistent with iter68/69 behaviour.

utils/statutory_bulk.py:57-101 (build_pf_ecr_txt) — correctly filters rows without pf_applicable OR without 12-digit numeric UAN. Empty output is b'' (no trailing newline) which matches EPFO's requirement. NCP days computation uses month_days - present_days - 0.5*half_days and clamps at ≥0.

utils/statutory_bulk.py:171-216 (build_esic_ip_reg_csv) — filters correctly: excludes rows with existing esi_ip_no OR esic_applicable=False. Falls back to father_name → nominee_name and dob → nominee_dob when the nominee master fields are blank, which matches the ESIC v2 template expectation.

Suggestion (LOW): the PF ECR builder currently ORs `gross_paid` and `monthly_gross` for the GROSS field (line 73). If neither is set (edge case for a partial run) both come out as 0, which the EPFO portal will accept but a manual reviewer might find suspicious. Consider logging a warning when both are missing so operators can spot bad data upstream. Non-blocking.

success_rate.backend: 100% (26/26 iter70 tests + 36/38 iter68+69 regression — the 2 skips are pre-existing env-data limitations, not iter70 regressions)

seed_data_creation: Two test employees created under SKSCO1 (name prefix TEST_iter70_*, random phones +919999x/+919998x). Both auto-deleted (users doc + attendance records) in the fixture teardown via direct pymongo delete_many. Verified post-run: 0 TEST_iter70 users remain. During test_c the SKSCO1 office_lat/lng were $unset temporarily then restored to the original (28.4089, 77.3178); verified restored post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 70 (biometric geofence gate + 3 statutory bulk endpoints) fully verified — all 26 tests green, iter68/69 regression 36/38 (2 pre-existing skips). Test file is self-contained and uses direct pymongo for (a) cleanup of TEST_iter70_* users and (b) the temporary $unset of SKSCO1 geofence in test_c (properly restored in a try/finally). To rerun everything for iter68+69+70: `pytest backend/tests/test_iter68_admin_employee_and_pdfs.py backend/tests/test_iter69_bulk_payslips_and_annual.py backend/tests/test_iter69_salary_register_empty_rows.py backend/tests/test_iter70_geofence_and_statutory.py -v`. NOTE: The only compliance_salary_run in the preview env (csrun_5b9d1e339e75) has rows=[] and company_id=None; row-level output verification is therefore done at the builder level (build_pf_ecr_txt / build_esic_mc_csv / build_esic_ip_reg_csv called directly with synthetic rows). If main agent later seeds a real SKSCO1 compliance run, the endpoint tests will still work — none of them assume non-empty rows.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found in iter 70 code paths. Biometric geofence gate correctly enforces the geofence when firm has office_lat/lng set and client sent coords + selfie, and correctly falls back to legacy biometric-only mode when firm has no geofence. All three statutory bulk endpoints emit the specified content-type, filename, headers/rows, and enforce cross-firm 403 + 404-on-missing.
